channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Canada-India ties could take a long time to recover
item.description: NEW DELHI (AP) — A diplomatic row that has strained bilateral relations between India and Canada for over a year has boiled over as the countries expelled each other’s top diplomats over the killing of a Sikh activist in Canada and allegations of other crimes.
item.dc:creator: SHEIKH SAALIQ, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: North Korea blows up parts of inter-Korean road and rail links in a symbolic display of anger
item.description: In a move that underlines North Korea's growing anger with South Korea, North Korea has blown up sections of unused rail routes and roads that once connected the two countries.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hezbollah vows to expand attacks in Israel after deadly strike in northern Lebanon
item.description: AITO, Lebanon (AP) — One day after a deadly Israeli airstrike in northern Lebanon – far from Hezbollah’s main area of influence in the south and east – the acting leader of the militant group said it would aim rockets into more areas of Israel.
item.dc:creator: KAREEM CHEHAYEB and BASSEM MROUE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US warns Israel to boost humanitarian aid into Gaza or risk losing weapons funding
item.description: The Biden administration has warned Israel that it must increase the amount of humanitarian aid it is allowing into Gaza within the next 30 days or it could risk losing access to U.S. weapons funding.
item.dc:creator: Lolita C. Baldor, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Russia, China collaborating with criminal networks in cyberattacks against adversaries: Microsoft
item.description: Russia, China and Iran are increasingly collaborating with cybercrime networks to launch a variety of attacks against their adversaries, including the U.S., Microsoft said in a Tuesday report.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Egypt's long-delayed mega-museum is opening some galleries in a trial run
item.description: Officials say the long-delayed Grand Egyptian Museum will partially open its main galleries on Wednesday, showcasing 12 halls that exhibit aspects of ancient Egypt.
item.dc:creator: Ahmed Hatem, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Giant pandas arrive at National Zoo
item.description: Two giant pandas arrived at the National Zoo in Washington, D.C. on Tuesday after a flight from China, officials said.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Iran terror proxies amass on Israel’s borders in 'Ring of Fire’
item.description: Iran has developed the so called "Ring of Fire" around Israel to further expand its influence throughout the region. Experts note that violent terror groups sit across the Jewish state's borders.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Committee investigating Indian assassination plot meeting officials in Washington
item.description: An Indian government committee investigating Indian involvement in a foiled murder plot against a prominent activist in New York City will meet U.S. officials in Washington this week, the State Department announced.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Kenya's High Court rejects move to stop deputy president's impeachment debate
item.description: Kenya’s High Court has rejected an application by the deputy president’s lawyers to stop the senate from debating an impeachment motion against him after parliament voted to remove him from office last week.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: A Hong Kong zoo seeks answers after 9 monkeys die in 2 days
item.description: HONG KONG (AP) — Hong Kong's oldest zoo is seeking answers in a monkey medical mystery after nine animals died in two days, including three members of a critically endangered species.
item.dc:creator: KANIS LEUNG, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Hereditary nobles have sat in Britain's Parliament for centuries. Their time may be up
item.description: British lawmakers are voting on a bill to strip hereditary aristocrats of the right to sit and vote in the House of Lords after more than 700 years.
item.dc:creator: Jill Lawless, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Violence-hit Pakistan locks down the capital for an Asian security meeting
item.description: ISLAMABAD (AP) — Shaken by multiple militant attacks, Pakistani authorities have locked down the capital in a major security move as senior officials from several nations arrive for an Asian security group meeting.
item.dc:creator: MUNIR AHMED, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Greek official accuses EU of policy failure on migration as war and climate change fuel displacement
item.description: ATHENS, Greece (AP) — A Greek official on Tuesday criticized the outgoing European Commission for failing to forge a common policy to deport migrants, and she warned that war and climate change were increasing global displacement.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: North Korea blows up parts of inter-Korean road and rail links in a symbolic display of anger
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — In a symbolic display of anger, North Korea on Tuesday blew up the northern sections of unused road and rail routes that once linked it with South Korea, with the rivals exchanging threats days after the North claimed that the South flew drones over its capital Pyongyang.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM and KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Father called UK police to confess to killing daughter, 10, in England after he fled to Pakistan
item.description: The father of a 10-year-old girl who was found dead in England called U.K. police, after flying to Pakistan, to say that he had killed his daughter, prosecutors said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A pastry brought to Mexico by British miners is still popular after 200 years
item.description: REAL DEL MONTE, Mexico (AP) — Isabel Arriaga Lozano carefully fills a small pastry with a savory mix of meat, potatoes and chili pepper. She is crafting a “paste” (pronounced PAH-stay), a beloved Mexican snack with a rich history.
item.dc:creator: INDIA GRANT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: China deploys record 125 warplanes in large-scale military drill in warning to Taiwan
item.description: TAIPEI, Taiwan (AP) — China employed a record 125 aircraft, as well as its Liaoning aircraft carrier and ships, in large-scale military exercises surrounding Taiwan and its outlying islands Monday, simulating the sealing off of key ports in a move that underscores the tense situation in the Taiwan S
item.dc:creator: JOHNSON LAI and HUIZHONG WU, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Campaigning begins for Japan's parliamentary election
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — Official campaigning for Japan's Oct. 27 parliamentary election began Tuesday with new Prime Minister Shigeru Ishiba seeking a mandate for his policies and for reforms after the governing party’s political funds scandal.
item.dc:creator: MARI YAMAGUCHI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israeli strike in northern Lebanon kills at least 21 people
item.description: AITO, Lebanon (AP) — An Israeli airstrike hit an apartment building in northern Lebanon on Monday, killing at least 21 people, according to the Lebanese Red Cross. The Israeli military did not immediately comment and the target was not clear.
item.dc:creator: YAHYA HABCHITI, KAREEM CHEHAYEB, and WAFAA SHURAFA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: UN Security Council voices 'strong concern' for UN peacekeepers after Israeli attacks
item.description: UNITED NATIONS (AP) — The U.N. Security Council expressed “strong concern” Monday as Israel has fired on and wounded U.N. peacekeepers in southern Lebanon during intensified fighting, reiterating its support for their role in supporting security in the region. It's the first statement by the U.N.
item.dc:creator: EDITH M. LEDERER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israeli forces seize documents that reveal Hamas plan for more elaborate attacks: report
item.description: Hundreds of documents seized by Israeli forces reveal a broader ambition by Hamas to plot attacks against Israel in the years before Oct. 7, 2023.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hezbollah's drones are a fierce and evasive threat to Israel
item.description: TEL AVIV, Israel (AP) — One of the worst mass casualty strikes on Israel in a year of war came not from dozens of Iranian ballistic missiles nor the repeated barrages of rocket fire launched by Hamas and Hezbollah. Instead, it was a single drone.
item.dc:creator: TIA GOLDENBERG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Poland's leader defends his decision to suspend the right to asylum
item.description: WARSAW, Poland (AP) — Poland's Prime Minister Donald Tusk on Monday defended a plan to suspend the right to asylum as human rights and civil society organizations argued that fundamental rights must be respected.
item.dc:creator: VANESSA GERA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Vial that contained nerve agent that killed UK woman contained enough poison to kill thousands
item.description: LONDON (AP) — The lead counsel for a public inquiry into the 2018 death of a British woman poisoned by a Soviet-developed nerve agent said Monday that there was enough poison in the vial she unwittingly opened to kill thousands of people.
item.dc:creator: DANICA KIRKA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: UNIFIL ignored Hezbollah terror build up for 18 years, Israel's UN ambassador says
item.description: Amid fighting between Israel and Hezbollah terrorists, the U.N. is facing criticism that its peacekeeping force has failed to stop the Iran-terror proxy's military build up in southern Lebanon.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US raises concern over Chinese military drills near Taiwan
item.description: The United States said it is “seriously concerned” about Chinese military drills near Taiwan on Monday. State Department spokesperson Matthew Miller said the U.S. is concerned by the joint military drills the People’s Liberation Army is conducting in the Taiwan Strait.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: North Koreans fighting in Ukraine alongside Russians, Zelensky says
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky said Sunday that North Korean troops are now fighting alongside Russian forces in Ukraine, appearing to confirm reports that have been circulating in recent weeks.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, The Hill

item.title: China surrounds Taiwan in military exercise against independence
item.description: China has launched massive military exercises surrounding Taiwan in response to the island refusing to follow the rule of the Communist Party.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: North Korea is preparing to destroy northern parts of inter-Korean roads, Seoul says
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korea said Monday it has detected signs that North Korea is preparing to destroy the northern parts of inter-Korean roads no longer in use, as the rivals are embroiled in soaring tensions over North Korea’s claim that South Korea flew drones over its territory.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM and KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Kremlin confirms Trump sent COVID tests to Putin during pandemic
item.description: “We also sent equipment at the beginning of the pandemic,” Kremlin spokesman Dmitry Peskov said. “But about the phone calls — it’s not true.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, NewsNation

item.title: Google loses final EU court appeal against 2.4 billion euro fine in antitrust shopping case
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Google lost its final legal challenge on Tuesday against a European Union penalty for giving its own shopping recommendations an illegal advantage over rivals in search results, ending a long-running antitrust case that came with a whopping fine.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: FBI arrests Afghan man who officials say planned Election Day attack in the US
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The FBI has arrested an Afghan man who officials say was inspired by the Islamic State militant organization and was plotting an Election Day attack targeting large crowds in the U.S., the Justice Department said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Musk's X to be reinstated in Brazil after complying with Supreme Court demands
item.description: SAO PAULO (AP) — The Brazilian Supreme Court's Justice Alexandre de Moraes on Tuesday authorized the restoration of social media platform X´s service in Brazil, over a month after its nationwide shutdown, according to a court document that was made public. Elon Musk’s X was blocked on Aug.
item.dc:creator: GABRIELA SÁ PESSOA and BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, NewsNation
